title: Records, Firsts, and Oddities
description: A tougher trivia set about milestones, unusual facts, and notable firsts.

questions.prompt: Who was the first person to walk on the Moon?
questions.hint: He spoke about one small step.
questions.explanation: Neil Armstrong became the first person to walk on the Moon in 1969. For further study, review the Apollo 11 mission.

questions.prompt: Which country gifted the Statue of Liberty to the United States?
questions.hint: The gift commemorated friendship across the Atlantic.
questions.explanation: France gave the Statue of Liberty to the United States. For further study, explore the statue's design and dedication history.

questions.prompt: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens.
questions.hint: The location connected the modern games to ancient Greece.
questions.explanation: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens in 1896. For further study, review the revival of the Olympics.

questions.prompt: The first artificial satellite launched into orbit was _____.
questions.hint: It was launched by the Soviet Union in 1957.
questions.explanation: Sputnik 1 was the first artificial satellite. For further study, review the beginning of the Space Age.

questions.prompt: Which animal is known for having fingerprints that can resemble human fingerprints?
questions.hint: It is a tree-dwelling marsupial from Australia.
questions.explanation: Koalas have fingerprints that can look surprisingly similar to human fingerprints. For further study, compare convergent traits in animals.

questions.prompt: Which element has the atomic number 1?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen has atomic number 1. For further study, review the structure of the periodic table.

questions.prompt: Which city hosted the first widely recognized modern World's Fair in 1851?
questions.hint: It was held in the Crystal Palace.
questions.explanation: London hosted the Great Exhibition of 1851. For further study, review World's Fairs and industrial-era display culture.

questions.prompt: Venus rotates in the opposite direction from most planets in the solar system.
questions.explanation: Venus has retrograde rotation compared with most planets. For further study, review planetary rotation and axial tilt.

questions.prompt: Which book is often cited as the best-selling novel of all time?
questions.hint: It was written by Miguel de Cervantes.
questions.explanation: 'Don Quixote' is often cited as the best-selling novel of all time. For further study, review early modern European literature.

questions.prompt: Which metal is liquid at room temperature?
questions.hint: It shares its name with a planet.
questions.explanation: Mercury is a metal that is liquid at room temperature. For further study, review states of matter and metallic elements.

questions.prompt: The world's largest coral reef system is the Great Barrier _____.
questions.hint: It lies off Australia's northeast coast.
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system. For further study, review reef ecosystems and marine biodiversity.

questions.prompt: Who was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean?
questions.explanation: Amelia Earhart was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean. For further study, review early aviation milestones.
